prompts.name: GNHF Interrupted Run Recovery
prompts.sprintPathRole: Preserve and continue an interrupted GNHF worktree
prompts.useThisWhen: A preserved run stopped because of restart, timeout, failed validation, or failed finalization.
prompts.doNotUseWhen: No preserved worktree exists or the run completed normally.
prompts.inspectFirst: Exact worktree, notes, gnhf.log, iteration evidence, Git state.
prompts.expectedOutput: Evidence-backed recovery and at most one continuation commit.
prompts.nextStep: Use P11/P12 after completion or P31 for commit-only repair.
prompts.acceptanceGate: No destructive cleanup; saved work is preserved and dispositioned.
prompts.authority: Preservation and continuation in the exact existing worktree
prompts.proofCeiling: No destructive cleanup; saved work is preserved and dispositioned.
prompts.text:
gnhf `
--agent opencode `
--current-branch `
--max-iterations 3 `
--max-tokens 240000 `
--prevent-sleep on `
--stop-when "The interrupted worktree state is understood, useful work is preserved, and the saved objective is either safely continued through one commit or stopped with an exact blocker." `
"Repo: xyz_repo_or_path

Sprint: Interrupted GNHF recovery
Lane: preserve, diagnose, continue

Run from:
- the exact preserved GNHF worktree

Objective:
Inspect the current branch, status, recent commits, notes, gnhf.log, and iteration evidence. Preserve useful work and complete the smallest unfinished owned item.

Owned scope:
- xyz_owned_scope

Forbidden:
- xyz_forbidden_scope
- reset hard, clean, worktree removal, duplicate implementation, push, merge, credentials

Rules:
- Do not repeat repository-wide discovery.
- Repair failed validation or commit readiness before new work.
- Keep unknown changes untouched and stop if ownership is unclear.
- Run xyz_canonical_proof after the repair.

Report:
- worktree and run
- interruption classification
- preserved work
- commit created
- validation
- remaining queue
- git status"

prompts.name: GNHF Single Mutation
prompts.useThisWhen: One concrete sprint should produce one coherent validated commit.
prompts.doNotUseWhen: The work contains several independent items; use P30.
prompts.inspectFirst: Owned scope, plan if present, direct implementation and proof surfaces.
prompts.expectedOutput: One tracked mutation, one commit, and focused validation.
prompts.nextStep: Use P11/P12 or another atomic lane.
prompts.acceptanceGate: One owned mutation is committed and validated.
prompts.authority: One tracked mutation and local GNHF commit
prompts.proofCeiling: One owned mutation is committed and validated.
prompts.text:
gnhf `
--agent opencode `
--worktree `
--max-iterations 3 `
--max-tokens 300000 `
--prevent-sleep on `
--stop-when "One bounded owned-scope mutation is committed and validated, or an exact external blocker proves that no safe mutation can proceed." `
"Repo: xyz_repo_or_path

Sprint: xyz_sprint_name
Lane: single bounded repository mutation

Objective:
Implement the highest-value evidence-backed change inside xyz_owned_scope. Produce one coherent commit and stop.

Context:
- Read AGENTS.md and xyz_plan_directory if present.
- Reuse existing patterns.
- Preserve unrelated work.

Proof:
- Run the narrow checks for the changed surface.
- Run xyz_canonical_proof when shared contracts change.
- Required failures remain failures.

Forbidden:
- xyz_forbidden_scope
- push, merge, release, credentials, live targets, destructive Git, generated evidence

Report:
- commit
- changed files
- commands and results
- proof ceiling
- blockers
- git status"

prompts.name: GNHF Repo-First Mutation
prompts.sprintPathRole: Recover compact repo truth and make the first safe mutation
prompts.useThisWhen: The repository is unfamiliar or stale but should move in the first run.
prompts.doNotUseWhen: The repo and exact mutation are already known; use P28.
prompts.inspectFirst: Repo rules, Git floor, recent commits, direct plans, canonical tests.
prompts.expectedOutput: At most three candidates and the first safe mutation commit.
prompts.acceptanceGate: First mutation occurs without repository-wide archaeology.
prompts.authority: Compact discovery followed by first tracked mutation
prompts.proofCeiling: First mutation occurs without repository-wide archaeology.
prompts.text:
gnhf `
--agent opencode `
--worktree `
--max-iterations 3 `
--max-tokens 300000 `
--prevent-sleep on `
--stop-when "The repository floor is understood and the first safe evidence-backed mutation is committed and validated." `
"Repo: xyz_repo_or_path

Sprint: Repo-first safe mutation
Lane: compact discovery plus action

Objective:
Recover only enough repository truth to select and complete the first safe high-value mutation.

Inspect:
- AGENTS.md
- branch, HEAD, status, recent commits
- xyz_plan_directory if present
- canonical tests and validators
- generated-artifact rules

Candidate limit:
- at most three
- rank broken proof, correctness, safety, regression coverage, repeatability, operator friction

Execution:
- Choose one candidate.
- Modify tracked files in xyz_owned_scope during the first iteration.
- Run targeted checks and xyz_canonical_proof when relevant.
- Do not expand into a roadmap.

Forbidden:
- xyz_forbidden_scope
- broad redesign, dependency churn, push, credentials, live targets, destructive Git

Report:
- candidates
- selected mutation
- commit
- validation
- proof ceiling
- git status"

prompts.name: GNHF Finite Queue
prompts.sprintPathRole: Execute a supplied queue of one to five items
prompts.useThisWhen: A small ordered backlog is already known.
prompts.doNotUseWhen: The queue is open-ended or larger than five active items.
prompts.inspectFirst: Supplied queue, dependencies, owned paths, canonical proof.
prompts.expectedOutput: One coherent commit per item and final disposition ledger.
prompts.nextStep: Use P11/P12 or P27 after interruption.
prompts.acceptanceGate: Every supplied item is dispositioned and required proof passes.
prompts.authority: Tracked mutations limited to the supplied queue
prompts.proofCeiling: Every supplied item is dispositioned and required proof passes.
prompts.text:
gnhf `
--agent opencode `
--worktree `
--max-iterations 6 `
--max-tokens 600000 `
--prevent-sleep on `
--stop-when "Every supplied queue item is completed, blocked with evidence, superseded, or rejected, and all changed surfaces pass their required checks." `
"Repo: xyz_repo_or_path

Sprint: Finite queue execution
Lane: ordered bounded mutations

Queue:
- xyz_bounded_queue

Owned scope:
- xyz_owned_scope

Objective:
Execute the supplied queue in dependency order. Make one coherent commit per item.

Rules:
- Keep no more than five active items.
- Add only a prerequisite strictly required by an existing item.
- Do not add unrelated ideas.
- Record each item as completed, blocked, superseded, or rejected.
- Run targeted proof per item.
- Run xyz_canonical_proof after shared contract changes.
- Required failures remain failures.

Forbidden:
- xyz_forbidden_scope
- push, merge, release, credentials, live targets, destructive Git, generated evidence

Report:
- normalized queue
- item dispositions
- ordered commits
- validation by item
- blockers
- git status"

prompts.name: GNHF Commit Finalization Repair
prompts.sprintPathRole: Repair and commit an existing unfinished mutation
prompts.useThisWhen: A worktree already contains an intended change that failed validation or commit finalization.
prompts.inspectFirst: Current diff, ownership, narrow failing check, shared proof when relevant.
prompts.expectedOutput: The existing change is committed or preserved with an exact blocker.
prompts.acceptanceGate: No new feature begins; existing work reaches a known commit state.
prompts.authority: Existing diff repair and commit finalization only
prompts.proofCeiling: No new feature begins; existing work reaches a known commit state.
prompts.text:
gnhf `
--agent opencode `
--current-branch `
--max-iterations 2 `
--max-tokens 180000 `
--prevent-sleep on `
--stop-when "The existing uncommitted change is either validated and committed coherently or preserved with an exact reason that commit finalization is unsafe." `
"Repo: xyz_repo_or_path

Sprint: Commit finalization repair
Lane: existing work only

Run from:
- the exact worktree containing the unfinished change

Objective:
Inspect the current diff and finish only the existing intended mutation. Repair its narrow validation or commit-readiness defect, then commit it.

Owned scope:
- files already changed
- directly required tests or validators

Rules:
- Do not start a new feature.
- Do not discard or rewrite unrelated changes.
- Confirm ownership of every modified file.
- Run the narrow failing check and xyz_canonical_proof when shared contracts changed.
- Do not use reset hard, clean, push, merge, credentials, or live targets.

Report:
- starting diff
- repair
- commit
- commands and results
- remaining uncommitted files
- proof ceiling
- git status"
